name: Computing & Data Infringement
description: Infringement scoring overrides for Computing & Data patents. Covers software architecture, databases, cloud computing, and general-purpose computing.
scoringGuidance: Software patents describe methods and systems at a high abstraction level. Focus on whether the product implements the same algorithmic steps or system architecture.
Cloud service documentation often reveals more about internal architecture than traditional product docs — API references, architecture guides, and white papers are valuable.
Database and data retrieval patents use formal terms that map to well-known query processing, indexing, and storage patterns in every database product.
Virtualization and container patents apply broadly — any cloud or enterprise product using VMs or containers inherently practices many computing patents.
necessaryImplicationGuidance: Any cloud service necessarily implements: (1) resource scheduling, (2) multi-tenancy isolation, (3) API gateway/authentication, (4) monitoring/logging. Any database product necessarily implements: (1) query parsing/optimization, (2) indexing, (3) transaction management, (4) buffer/cache management.
